---
title: "Code Collapse Demonstration"
output:
  html_document:
    code_folding: hide
---

## Checking 1st and 2nd Order Trends

-   No need to remove trends from data as the $R^2$ for both $1^{st}$ and $2^{nd}$ explain less than 1% of the variance in the data

```{r trend-analysis, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(gstat)
library(sf)
library(sp)

# Load data
hawaii_sf <- st_read("./Hawaii_data/Hawaii.geojson", quiet = TRUE)
gao_sf <- st_read("./GAO_point_data/2016_htmasked_brown_clipped_centers.geojson", quiet = TRUE)

gao_sp <- as(gao_sf, "Spatial")

# Identify your target column
target <- "Area"

# Ensure it is numeric (Requirement for dissimilarity calculations)
gao_sp[[target]] <- as.numeric(gao_sp[[target]])

# Extract coordinates to X and Y columns
gao_sp$X <- coordinates(gao_sp)[,1]
gao_sp$Y <- coordinates(gao_sp)[,2]

# Check 1st and 2nd order trends
trend_check <- lm(Area ~ X + Y, data = as.data.frame(gao_sp))
summary(trend_check)

trend_check_sq <- lm(Area ~ X + Y + I(X^2) + I(Y^2) + I(X*Y), data = as.data.frame(gao_sp))
summary(trend_check_sq)

```

## Canopy Spatial Structure

```{r canopy-variogram, message=FALSE}
library(terra)

# Load Tree Canopy Cover (TCC)
tcc_2017 <- rast("./Hawaii_data/2017_tcc_hawaii.tif")

# Set zero values to NA directly on the raster
tcc_2017[tcc_2017 == 0] <- NA

# 1. Take your sample (na.rm = TRUE now safely handles the newly minted NAs)
tcc_sample <- spatSample(tcc_2017, size = 20000, method = "random", na.rm = TRUE, xy = TRUE)

# 2. RENAME the column immediately
names(tcc_sample)[3] <- "canopy"

# 3. Clean and convert
tcc_clean <- na.omit(tcc_sample)
tcc_sf <- st_as_sf(tcc_clean, coords = c("x", "y"), crs = crs(tcc_2017))

# 4. Run the variogram
vgm_tcc <- variogram(canopy ~ 1, 
                     data = tcc_sf, 
                     cutoff = 1600, 
                     width = 30)

plot(vgm_tcc, main = "Canopy Spatial Structure: Hawaii")
```

## Generating Pseudo-Absences (Negatives)

```{r generate-negatives, fig.width=10, fig.height=10, fig.align='center'}
# Set up the 2x2 plotting grid
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))

# ==========================================
# STEP 1: Density Map to Survey Polygon (Figure 1)
# ==========================================
density_300m <- rast("./GAO_point_data/2016_htmasked_brown_clipped_300m_countha.tif")
footprint <- density_300m * 0 + 1
survey_poly <- as.polygons(footprint) # The broad flight path

terra::plot(density_300m, main = "Step 1: 300m Density Map")

# ==========================================
# STEP 2: Clip TCC by Survey & Create Forest Polygon (Figure 2)
# ==========================================
# 1. Project boundary to match TCC (assuming tcc_2017 exists)
survey_poly_tcc <- project(survey_poly, crs(tcc_2017))

# 2. Clip TCC to flight path and apply ZERO filtering logic
tcc_clipped <- terra::crop(tcc_2017, survey_poly_tcc)
tcc_clipped <- terra::mask(tcc_clipped, survey_poly_tcc)
tcc_clipped[tcc_clipped <= 0] <- NA # <--- Keep zero filtering logic

# 3. Turn the cleaned TCC (Forest only) into a Polygon
forest_footprint <- tcc_clipped * 0 + 1
forest_poly <- as.polygons(forest_footprint)

terra::plot(tcc_clipped, main = "Step 2: 30m TCC (Filtered to Path)")

# ==========================================
# STEP 3: Sample Pseudo-Absences from Forest TCC (Figure 3)
# ==========================================
# Sample points directly from the forest TCC (no DEM needed)
neg_sample <- spatSample(tcc_clipped, size = 265734, method = "random", na.rm = TRUE, xy = TRUE)
names(neg_sample)[3] <- "canopy"
neg_sf <- st_as_sf(na.omit(neg_sample), coords = c("x", "y"), crs = crs(tcc_2017))
neg_sf$presence <- 0

point_count <- nrow(neg_sf)
print(paste("Plotting", point_count, "points!"))

# 2. Plot the canopy as the background (dynamically adding the count to the title)
terra::plot(tcc_clipped, main = paste("Step 3:", point_count, "Forest Sample Points"))

# 3. Plot ALL points on top using high transparency 
# pch = 16 (solid circle), cex = 0.2 (very small size)
# rgb(1, 1, 1, 0.05) creates a white color that is 95% transparent
plot(st_geometry(neg_sf), 
     add = TRUE, 
     pch = 16, 
     cex = 0.2, 
     col = rgb(0.5, 0, 0.5, alpha = 0.9))
# ==========================================
# STEP 4: Plot ROD Presence Points (Figure 4)
# ==========================================
# NOTE: Replace 'rod_sf' with the actual variable name of your ROD sf object.
# Uncomment the transform line if your rod points are not already in crs(tcc_2017)
gao_sf_proj <- sf::st_transform(gao_sf, crs = sf::st_crs(tcc_clipped))

# 2. Plot the base raster
terra::plot(tcc_clipped, main = "Step 4: ROD Presence Points")

# 3. Add the points
# Changed pch from "." to 16 (solid circle) and added cex for size control
plot(st_geometry(gao_sf_proj), 
     add = TRUE, 
     pch = 16, 
     cex = 0.4, 
     col = "red")
```

## Cross-Variogram Formulation

```{r cross-variogram}
# --- 1. Preparation & Extraction ---
# Project gao_sf to match tcc_2017 CRS if needed
gao_sf <- st_transform(gao_sf, crs(tcc_2017))
pos_sf <- gao_sf
pos_sf$presence <- 1

# Extract TCC values for presence points
pos_sf$canopy <- terra::extract(tcc_2017, pos_sf)[,2]
# neg_sf already has canopy from the sampling step above

# Remove NA values
pos_sf <- pos_sf[!is.na(pos_sf$canopy), ]
neg_sf <- neg_sf[!is.na(neg_sf$canopy), ]

# --- 2. Subset & Sample ---
set.seed(52)
n_samples <- min(5000, nrow(pos_sf), nrow(neg_sf))

pos_subset <- pos_sf[sample(nrow(pos_sf), n_samples), ]
neg_subset <- neg_sf[sample(nrow(neg_sf), n_samples), ]

# --- 3. Align & Merge ---
pos_final <- pos_subset[, c("presence", "canopy")]
neg_final <- neg_subset[, c("presence", "canopy")]

# Ensure column geometry names match exactly before rbind
st_geometry(neg_final) <- "geometry"

combined_sf <- rbind(pos_final, neg_final)

# Validate the merge
table(combined_sf$presence) # Should show an even split of 1s and 0s

# --- 4. Run the Cross-Variogram ---
g_new <- gstat(NULL, id = "rod", formula = presence ~ 1, data = combined_sf)
g_new <- gstat(g_new, id = "canopy", formula = canopy ~ 1, data = combined_sf)

vgm_cross_new <- variogram(g_new, cutoff = 1500, width = 60)

plot(vgm_cross_new, main = "Cross-Spatial Structure (30m Lag Bins)")

g_residual <- gstat(NULL, id = "residual", formula = presence ~ canopy, data = combined_sf)
vgm_residual <- variogram(g_residual, cutoff = 3000, width = 30)
plot(vgm_residual, main = "presence ~ canopy residual variogram (30m Lag Bins)")
```
